teamObjective.explanation: As the opening leader with a weak hand, I want to start a trick that my partner can potentially win while I retain my slightly higher cards for future leads or responses. Opponent player 1 is down to 2 cards, so claiming tricks quickly helps our team avoid being pinned.

partnerBelief.summary: My partner (player 2, 3 cards) has not acted, so their hand is unknown. Given the hand counts, they may hold mid-to-high cards or trumps that can overtake a low club lead. I cannot assume specific cards; I only know they have 3 cards and have made no public plays.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponent player 1 has only 2 cards and is likely trying to finish, so they may conserve or play strong cards. Opponent player 3 has 3 cards and is also unknown. Neither has played any cards, so any inference about their exact holdings remains probabilistic.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the lowest single (club 3) is the standard hand-management play. It gives my partner the cheapest chance to win the trick with almost any higher card, and it preserves my 4, 5, and 6 for later tricks or responses. Since all my cards are low non-trumps, the absolute rank is the only differentiator, and keeping higher cards maximizes future options.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Playing the 4 instead of the 3 expends a slightly higher card now without adding any meaningful trick-winning power, leaving a weaker remaining hand.
The 5 is middle-of-the-range; leading it now still loses to almost any non-trump or trump higher card and gives up a card that might win a future trick against the 3 or 4.
Leading the highest card leaves me with only 3-4-5, reducing my ability to lead or respond effectively in upcoming tricks.

risks: Any opponent or my partner with a card higher than 3 (or any trump) will win the trick, so I may immediately lose the lead.
Opponent player 1 is near finish and could use a strong card to win and escape.
riskAssessment.mitigation: By leading the weakest card I minimize the value I give away if I lose the lead and keep my better cards to fight for future tricks.
